narrative: В 14:00 UTC нагрузка на платежный контур начала расти быстрее привычного дневного профиля, и первые признаки деградации появились в api-gateway. Сначала команда увидела рост tail latency, который выглядел как типичный всплеск, но уже через несколько минут задержки перестали снижаться. В payments-svc постепенно увеличивалась частота полных сборок мусора, а время пауз стало влиять на обработку синхронных запросов. По мере роста heap occupancy сервис чаще блокировался на выделении памяти, и в логах накапливались сигналы Allocation Failure. К 14:20 UTC upstream-ошибки в gateway выросли до уровня, заметного для пользователей на этапе авторизации платежа. В 14:30 UTC произошел первый подтвержденный OOM, после которого kubelet завершил контейнер payments-svc с типичным exit code 137. Автоматический рестарт с прежним memory limit не дал устойчивого результата, потому что рабочая нагрузка осталась высокой. На следующем цикле сервис снова вошел в критическую зону памяти и повторил падение, усилив нестабильность в смежных сервисах. api-gateway начал отдавать смесь 5xx и timeout, а retry-политика усилила давление на уже деградирующий payments-svc. order-svc зафиксировал устойчивую неуспешность dependency-вызовов и перевел circuit breaker в OPEN для ограничения каскада. Это защитило часть системы от перегрузки, но одновременно временно заблокировало платежно-зависимые пользовательские сценарии. В 14:56 UTC был подтвержден CrashLoopBackOff, что совпало с алертом KubePodCrashLooping и окончательно зафиксировало масштаб инцидента. Онколл провел диагностику и в 14:52–14:58 UTC применил явный фикс: увеличил memory limit deployment-а и скорректировал JVM headroom. После релиза новой конфигурации свежий pod стартовал без мгновенного OOM, а профиль GC стал значительно стабильнее. С 15:00 UTC gateway-ошибки начали устойчиво снижаться, а circuit breaker в order-svc перешел через HALF_OPEN к CLOSED. К 15:10 UTC инцидент был операционно закрыт как восстановленный, хотя команда оставила задачи на capacity planning и алертинг. Связка временных маркеров, алертов и лого-сигналов делает memory exhaustion в payments-svc наиболее убедительной первопричиной.

events.description: Before the hard failure, payments-svc showed sustained full GC pauses, rising old-gen occupancy, and longer request execution tails, indicating that heap headroom was shrinking under real checkout traffic and that latency degradation was tied to memory pressure rather than to a short network fluctuation.

kubelet emitted an OOMKilling event for payments-svc and terminated the Java process with exit code 137, which directly interrupted payment processing and marked the point where degradation transitioned into a concrete service outage with customer-visible failures.

The gateway observed a sustained spike in upstream 5xx and timeout responses from payments-svc, and the retry layer amplified load against an already unstable dependency, increasing visible error rates and extending the practical blast radius of the incident.

order-svc opened its circuit breaker for the payments dependency after crossing configured failure thresholds, which reduced downstream pressure but temporarily blocked payment-dependent order completion paths and converted live calls into controlled rejects.

The on-call engineer patched the deployment to raise container memory from 2Gi to 4Gi and aligned JVM sizing to preserve heap headroom, introducing the first remediation step that directly targeted the observed failure mode instead of only restarting pods.

After the new pod stabilized and gateway errors dropped, order-svc transitioned circuit breaker states from OPEN through HALF_OPEN to CLOSED, confirming recovery of the dependency path and restoration of normal payment-related order flow handling.

causal_chains.description: Progressive heap saturation and long GC pauses in payments-svc culminated in OOM kill by kubelet, converting performance degradation into a hard outage condition.
Primary OOM failure in payments-svc propagated upstream errors to gateway and then forced order-svc to open circuit breaker as a protection mechanism.
Raising memory limits and rebalancing JVM capacity stabilized pod behavior and enabled dependent services to close breaker states and return to normal operations.

hypotheses.title: Memory exhaustion is the root cause
hypotheses.description: Cross-layer evidence from JVM logs, kubelet OOM events, and incident timing strongly indicates that payments-svc memory exhaustion is the primary root cause. The degradation pattern starts with GC amplification, advances to allocation failures, and ends with OOM-kill cycles that directly map to customer-facing errors. The fact that service stability returns after increasing container memory and adjusting JVM headroom materially strengthens causality versus alternative theories.

hypotheses.title: Retry and breaker dynamics amplified impact
hypotheses.description: Even with OOM as the root trigger, system-wide impact was amplified by gateway retry behavior and order-svc protection transitions. Retries increased pressure on a failing dependency, while breaker OPEN periods expanded temporary feature unavailability for payment-linked order flows. This hypothesis explains why the incident severity exceeded what a single pod crash would normally produce and why recovery required both capacity fix and downstream stabilization.

anomalies.description: OOM, gateway error spike, and breaker OPEN appeared in a tightly coupled sequence, showing unusually fast cascade propagation between three services.

gaps.description: Heap dump and fine-grained allocation traces were not retained before first OOM, limiting precise attribution between leak-like growth and workload burst effects.

impact_summary: From 14:30 to approximately 15:10 UTC, payment operations experienced elevated failure probability due to repeated OOM and restart instability in payments-svc. Gateway clients observed increased 5xx and timeout responses, while order-svc entered protection mode that temporarily constrained payment-dependent order completion. Recovery required an explicit memory capacity fix, after which service health, breaker state, and user-visible error rates returned to acceptable ranges.
preliminary_recommendations: Raise payments-svc memory limit and align JVM Xmx with safe headroom
Add early alerts on heap >80% and long GC pause before OOM
Cap gateway retries for failing payment upstream during incident mode
